concepts.title: IT Act Definition
concepts.description: The Information Technology Act, 2000 is a law passed by the Indian Parliament to regulate the use of technology, e-commerce, and cybersecurity in the country. It provides a framework for electronic governance, digital signatures, and cybersecurity, as well as penalties for cybercrimes.

concepts.title: Cyber Crime Categories
concepts.description: Cybercrimes can be broadly categorized into three types: (1) Cyber-enabled crimes (e.g., hacking, identity theft), (2) Cyber-dependent crimes (e.g., online fraud, cyberstalking), and (3) Cyber-specific crimes (e.g., cyber terrorism, cyber extortion).

concepts.title: Cyber Crime Penalties
concepts.description: The IT Act provides for severe penalties for cybercrimes, including imprisonment up to three years and fines up to Rs. 5 lakhs for first-time offenders. Repeat offenders can face imprisonment up to five years and fines up to Rs. 10 lakhs.

concepts.title: Cyber Security Measures
concepts.description: To remember the basic cyber security measures, use the mnemonic 'CARE': (1) C - Confidentiality (protecting sensitive information), (2) A - Authenticity (verifying the identity of users and systems), (3) R - Integrity (ensuring data accuracy and completeness), and (4) E - Availability (ensuring systems and data are accessible when needed).

concepts.title: Confusing Cybercrime with Cyber Terrorism
concepts.description: Cybercrime and cyber terrorism are often used interchangeably, but they are not the same. Cybercrime refers to any unlawful activity committed using technology, while cyber terrorism involves using technology to disrupt critical infrastructure or cause harm to individuals or groups.

concepts.title: IT Act Sections
concepts.description: Some key sections of the IT Act to remember include: Section 43 (Liability of persons responsible for causing damage by computers), Section 66 (Computer-related Offences), and Section 79 (Liability of intermediaries).

concepts.title: Cybercrime Investigation
concepts.description: Cybercrime investigations require specialized skills and tools, including digital forensics, network analysis, and cryptography. Investigators must also follow legal procedures to ensure evidence is admissible in court.

concepts.title: Cyber Security Best Practices
concepts.description: To protect against cyber threats, individuals and organizations should follow best practices such as using strong passwords, keeping software up-to-date, using antivirus software, and being cautious of phishing emails and attachments.
